sourceMovie.title: Okja
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Okja for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Bong Joon Ho keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Okja clicked for you, Rise of the Planet of the Apes (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Okja?
faq.answer: Try Rise of the Planet of the Apes, Dawn of the Planet of the Apes, Monsters vs Aliens—each shares DNA with Okja while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Okja?
faq.answer: Okja (2017) is best described as Adventure, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Okja worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
